channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Dandora Mourns Matatu Conductor Killed in Stabbing, Police Pursue Repeat Offender
item.description: Residents of Dandora, Nairobi, are in mourning after the brutal stabbing of 21-year-old Joseph Mwangi, a matatu conductor, on Saturday, April 4th. Mwangi was attacked approximately 100 metres from his...

item.title: Kenyan Sisters in UK Complete Family Home Project Worth KSh 8 Million in Eldoret
item.description: Faith Chemeli and Lucy Wilbers, two Kenyan sisters based in the United Kingdom, have touched hearts online after successfully building a KSh 8 million mansion for their parents in Eldoret, Uasin Gishu...

item.title: NASA's Artemis II Crew Successfully Launches for Historic Lunar Flyby
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission has successfully launched with four astronauts aboard the Orion capsule, marking a significant milestone in the United States' lunar exploration efforts. The spacecraft will ...

item.title: Balochistan Faces Devastating Rainfall: 11 Dead and Over a Dozen Injured
item.description: Heavy rainfall across Balochistan has led to the death of 11 people, with numerous others injured due to rain-related incidents. The most tragic losses occurred in Qila Abdullah and Kakar Khurasan dis...

item.title: Preview of the Men’s Basketball National Championship: UConn vs. Michigan
item.description: The men’s college basketball national championship is set as the University of Michigan faces the University of Connecticut in Indianapolis. Michigan, coached by Dusty May, enters the game as a strong...

item.title: Energy Expert Warns of Potential Global Shortages
item.description: Energy analyst Eric Nuttall is raising concerns about potential global energy shortages, suggesting that the situation could worsen significantly within days. He points to a combination of factors inc...

item.title: US-Israeli Airstrikes Hit Iran’s Petrochemical and Military Sites as Regional Conflict Escalates
item.description: A new round of US-Israeli airstrikes hit Iran’s critical energy and civilian infrastructure, escalating tensions that erupted after the 28th February attacks. The latest strikes targeted multiple petr...

item.title: Authorities Plan Removal of Volkswagen Beetle Suspended on Sacred Squamish Rock Face
item.description: A Volkswagen Beetle was recently discovered suspended on a rock face above Darrell Bay in Squamish, B.C., prompting safety concerns and cultural objections. The car, reportedly placed there by individ...

item.title: Heavy rains trigger landslides, shutting Jammu-Srinagar National Highway in Ramban district
item.description: Heavy rainfall on Sunday night caused massive landslides in Jammu and Kashmir’s Ramban district, blocking the strategic Jammu-Srinagar National Highway — the only all-weather road link connecting the ...

item.title: NASA's Artemis II Crew Discovers Unusual Lunar Features During Historic Flyby
item.description: NASA's Artemis II crew has achieved a significant milestone by completing the first human flyby of the moon’s far side. During the mission, astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and ...

item.title: Associated Press Introduces Buyouts to Focus on Digital and Visual Journalism
item.description: The Associated Press (AP) is initiating voluntary buyouts for its U.S.-based journalists as it transitions from a traditional newspaper-focused model to one emphasizing digital, broadcast, and visual ...

item.title: Artemis Mission Achieves Historic Lunar Flyby, Breaking New Ground for Space Exploration
item.description: A momentous occasion unfolded as the Artemis crew embarked on a historic lunar flyby, marking the first time human eyes have witnessed a vast expanse of the Moon’s surface, including the rarely seen O...

item.title: APC Restarts Nationwide Digital Membership Registration for Citizens
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) don announce say dem don resume their electronic membership registration for all states and wards across Nigeria. According to APC National Publicity Secretary, Mr ...

item.title: Government invests £1.2m to train schools in tackling knife crime
item.description: The British government has announced a £1.2 million investment to provide specialist training for up to 250 schools located in knife crime hotspots. The initiative, part of the Safety In and Around Sc...

item.title: Presidential Aspirant Rotimi Explains Reasons for Joining ADC Amid Leadership Challenges
item.description: Abayomi Rotimi, wey dey aspire to become president under the African Democratic Congress (ADC), don talk why e decide join the opposition party even though dem dey face leadership wahala ahead of 2027...

item.title: Middle East Conflict Updates: Trump Administration Responds
item.description: Ongoing tensions in the Middle East are dominating headlines as of April 7, 2026. The news, according to this report, appears to be focused on the reactions and statements from the Trump administratio...

item.title: Deadline for voter registration in AJK extended till April 13
item.description: The Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK) Election Commission has officially extended the deadline for voter registration and the revision of electoral rolls for the upcoming 2026 Legislative Assembly election...

item.title: Gauteng teen dies after suspected diving accident at Sharpeville municipal pool
item.description: A 15-year-old boy from Lethabong in Sebokeng, Tshwarelo (Tsoarelo) Likana, died after a suspected diving accident at the Sharpeville municipal swimming pool in the Vaal during the Easter weekend. Acco...

item.title: Supreme Court seeks legislative updates on acts of lasciviousness laws
item.description: The Philippine Supreme Court has urged Congress to amend existing laws concerning acts of lasciviousness, highlighting the need to better protect victims of sexual abuse, particularly minors. The high...

item.title: Transport Canada Seeks Public Input on the Dangers of Bright Vehicle Headlights
item.description: Transport Canada is conducting a survey to gather feedback on the increasing brightness of vehicle headlights, a trend that is raising concerns about road safety. Over the years, LED headlights have b...
